previous_turns.query: How many vacation days do I have left this year?
previous_turns.answer: You have 8 vacation days remaining for 2025.

previous_turns.query: Can I carry any over to next year?
previous_turns.answer: Yes, you can carry over up to 5 days under the 2025 policy.

input.query: Great — so if I take 3 days off in December, how many will I carry over? Also, what happens to them if I leave the company?

candidates.title: PTO Policy 2025
candidates.content_preview: Carry-over limit is 5 days. Upon separation, unused accrued PTO is paid out at your final base rate.

candidates.title: Employee Separation Policy
candidates.content_preview: Accrued PTO balance is paid out within 30 days of last day of employment.

candidates.content: Turn 1: User has 8 vacation days remaining in 2025.
Turn 2: User confirmed carry-over limit is 5 days under 2025 policy.
Turn from 2024 session: User had 12 days remaining in 2024.
candidates.stale_reason: 2024 session data, not relevant to 2025 balance

reasoning_steps.content: Multi-part query: (1) calculate carry-over if taking 3 days, (2) separation PTO payout. Have prior turn context: 8 days remaining, 5-day carry-over limit.
Calling calculate_pto_balance: 8 - 3 = 5 days remaining, all 5 eligible for carry-over.
Retrieving separation policy for PTO payout rules.
Answering both questions using memory from turns 1-2 plus current retrieval.

output.final_answer:
If you take 3 days in December, you'll have exactly 5 days remaining — which is the maximum carry-over allowed, so you can carry all 5 over to 2026.

If you leave the company, any unused accrued PTO is paid out at your final base rate within 30 days of your last day.

expected_answer: 5 days carry-over (8 - 3 = 5, at the carry-over limit). PTO is paid out on separation within 30 days.
